L1.area: XML document parsing and resource descriptor processing

L2.description: An XML external entity (XXE) vulnerability exists where an XML parser does not disable external entity processing or network access when loading user-supplied XML input, potentially allowing an attacker to read local files or perform server-side request forgery (SSRF) via crafted XML documents.

L3.area: XRD (Extensible Resource Descriptor) XML document loading and parsing logic
L3.description: An XML external entity (XXE) vulnerability exists where the XML parser used to load XRD (Extensible Resource Descriptor) documents does not disable external entity processing or network access, allowing an attacker to supply crafted XML input containing external entity declarations that can read local files or perform server-side request forgery.

ground_truth.reason: The XML parser in the XRD document loading code does not disable external entity processing, allowing XXE attacks where crafted XML input can read local files or make network requests.
